description: In a right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two legs. If the legs are a and b and the hypotenuse is c, then a² + b² = c².
remark: A foundational result in Euclidean geometry; frequently used for distance computation and right-triangle solving.

description: A kite is a quadrilateral with two pairs of adjacent equal sides. Typical properties include: (1) two pairs of adjacent equal sides, (2) perpendicular diagonals, (3) one diagonal bisects the other, (4) one pair of opposite angles is equal, and (5) area S = 1/2 · d1 · d2, where d1 and d2 are diagonal lengths.
remark: Common in geometry proofs and problem-solving, especially involving diagonals, symmetry, and area computation.

remark: A primary first check for series convergence.

description: If g(x) ≤ f(x) ≤ h(x) near a, and lim(x→a) g(x) = lim(x→a) h(x) = L, then lim(x→a) f(x) = L.
remark: A commonly used technique in limit calculations.

description: For a composite function y = f(g(x)), the derivative is y' = f'(g(x))·g'(x).
remark: The most commonly used rule for differentiating composite functions.

description: For y = f(x)g(x), the derivative is y' = f'(x)g(x) + f(x)g'(x).
remark: Used for differentiating products of functions.
geometric_structure_data: schema=geo-kp-edge-1.0; unit=manim_unit; topic=Product_Rule; mode=hypothesized; source=hypothesized; confidence=0.60; steps=2; OBJ_1=formula_box; BOX:C(0,0,0),w=9.2,h=2.2; P: TL(-4.6,1.1,0), TR(4.6,1.1,0), BR(4.6,-1.1,0), BL(-4.6,-1.1,0); E: TOP(TL,TR,L=9.2,type=box_edge), RIGHT(TR,BR,L=2.2,type=box_edge), BOTTOM(BR,BL,L=9.2,type=box_edge), LEFT(BL,TL,L=2.2,type=box_edge); TEXT:A(0,0,0),align=center,content='(fg)′ = f′g + fg′'; OPS: create_box(C=0,0,0,w=9.2,h=2.2), place_text(A=0,0,0).

description: For y = f(x)/g(x) with g(x)≠0, the derivative is y' = (f'(x)g(x) − f(x)g'(x)) / [g(x)]².
remark: Used for differentiating quotients of functions.
geometric_structure_data: schema=geo-kp-edge-1.0; unit=manim_unit; topic=Quotient_Rule; mode=hypothesized; source=hypothesized; confidence=0.60; steps=2; OBJ_1=formula_box; BOX:C(0,0,0),w=9.2,h=2.3; P: TL(-4.6,1.15,0), TR(4.6,1.15,0), BR(4.6,-1.15,0), BL(-4.6,-1.15,0); E: TOP(TL,TR,L=9.2,type=box_edge), RIGHT(TR,BR,L=2.3,type=box_edge), BOTTOM(BR,BL,L=9.2,type=box_edge), LEFT(BL,TL,L=2.3,type=box_edge); TEXT:A(0,0,0),align=center,content='(f/g)′ = (f′g − fg′) / g²'; OPS: create_box(C=0,0,0,w=9.2,h=2.3), place_text(A=0,0,0).

description: If f(x)=x^n for a real constant n, then f'(x)=n·x^(n−1).
remark: One of the most basic differentiation rules.

description: To evaluate an integral of the form ∫ f(g(x))·g'(x) dx, set u=g(x). Then du=g'(x)dx and ∫ f(g(x))·g'(x) dx = ∫ f(u) du.
remark: One of the commonly used techniques in integration.
geometric_structure_data: schema=geo-kp-edge-1.0; unit=manim_unit; topic=u_Substitution_Substitution_Rule; mode=hypothesized; source=hypothesized; confidence=0.60; steps=2; OBJ_1=formula_box; BOX:C(0,0,0),w=11.2,h=2.6; P: TL(-5.6,1.3,0), TR(5.6,1.3,0), BR(5.6,-1.3,0), BL(-5.6,-1.3,0); E: TOP(TL,TR,L=11.2,type=box_edge), RIGHT(TR,BR,L=2.6,type=box_edge), BOTTOM(BR,BL,L=11.2,type=box_edge), LEFT(BL,TL,L=2.6,type=box_edge); TEXT:A(0,0,0),align=center,content='Let u=g(x), du=g′(x)dx ⇒ ∫ f(g(x))g′(x)dx = ∫ f(u)du'; OPS: create_box(C=0,0,0,w=11.2,h=2.6), place_text(A=0,0,0).

remark: Guarantees existence of global extrema on closed intervals; often used before optimization arguments.

description: If f has a local maximum or local minimum at an interior point c and f is differentiable at c, then f′(c)=0.
remark: Used to identify critical points as candidates for local extrema.

remark: Connects average rate of change to an instantaneous slope; foundational for many calculus results.

description: Newton's method approximates a root by iteration: x_{n+1} = x_n − f(x_n)/f′(x_n). It uses the tangent line at x_n to estimate the next approximation.
remark: Widely used in numerical root-finding problems in science and engineering.

description: If f is continuous on [a,b], differentiable on (a,b), and f(a)=f(b), then there exists c in (a,b) such that f′(c)=0.
remark: A special case of the Mean Value Theorem; guarantees a horizontal tangent when endpoints share the same value.

description: At a critical point c where f′(c)=0, if f″(c)>0 then f has a local minimum at c; if f″(c)<0 then f has a local maximum at c.
remark: Classifies local extrema using concavity near a critical point.

description: Pappus's centroid theorems relate solids/surfaces of revolution to the path length traced by the centroid. For volume: V = A·(2πd). For surface area: S = L·(2πd), where d is the centroid distance to the axis, A is area of the region, and L is arc length of the curve.
